seo.title: Building Agentic RAG with Vercel AI SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build agentic rag with Vercel AI SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Agentic RAG with Vercel AI SDK enables multi-step retrieval agents that autonomously plan queries, fetch from multiple sources, and synthesize attributed responses in streaming UIs. However, complex agentic flows introduce risks like partial hallucinations in streams, unvalidated tool results, and inconsistent outputs across LLM providers. Vex middleware integration provides runtime verification for source faithfulness, hallucination detection, and query accuracy to ensure reliable production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: Agentic RAG Architecture with Vercel AI SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture begins with a user query routed to an agent loop via Vercel AI SDK's `generateText` or `streamText`. The agent plans retrieval queries using an LLM, executes parallel tool calls to vector stores (e.g., Pinecone, Supabase) and web search APIs, then synthesizes results with source attribution. Responses stream back to React Server Components, enabling real-time UI updates with citations.

Key components include the `createStreamableUI` for rendering partial results, tool definitions for retrieval (semantic search, keyword fallback), and a router to handle multi-turn reasoning. Edge functions host the API route, but complex planning loops risk timeouts without proper guardrails.

Vex intercepts `streamText` calls to verify each agent step: query planning against user intent, retrieval faithfulness to sources, and final synthesis for cross-source consistency.

risks.name: Streaming responses with partial hallucinations
risks.description: Agent generates plausible but incorrect facts mid-stream before final verification
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic similarity checks on streaming chunks against retrieved sources

risks.name: Tool call results not validated before display
risks.description: Retrieval tools return irrelevant or empty results streamed without filtering
risks.mitigation: Vex validates tool outputs for relevance and completeness before synthesis

risks.name: Inconsistent structured output across providers
risks.description: Different LLMs format citations/tools differently, breaking UI parsing
risks.mitigation: Vex normalizes structured outputs with schema enforcement

risks.name: Edge function timeouts on complex agent tasks
risks.description: Multi-step planning loops exceed Vercel edge limits (10s cold start)
risks.mitigation: Vex early termination + async verification offloads heavy checks
